Magistrates are powerful people in authority for the public good. They are ministers of God's providence, responsible for maintaining order and peace, especially in punishing wrongdoers and protecting those who do right. Good rulers and judges who intend to do well are guided by God; bad ones who intend to do evil are restrained by God. We should submit to the authority of God in the leaders whom his providence places over us. But when justice is turned away from what is right, no good can be expected. The wrong actions of public figures cause public harm.

It is difficult for people to receive honor and not become proud because of it. But all rulers on earth will die, and all their honor will be laid in the dust. God governs the world. There is a righteous God to whom we may go and on whom we may depend. This also relates to the kingdom of the Messiah. Considering the state of affairs in the world, we need to pray that the Lord Jesus would quickly rule over all nations in truth, righteousness, and peace.
